plssem 0.1.1
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Categorical Models with plssem
    • Interaction Models with plssem
    • Linear Models with plssem
    • Handling Missing Values with plssem
    • Multilevel Models with plssem
    • Ordinal Regression with plssem
    • Parallel Bootstrapping with plssem

Multilevel Models with plssem

Source: vignettes/multilevel-models.Rmd
multilevel-models.Rmd

This vignette shows examples of multilevel random slopes and intercept models, with both continuous and ordinal data.

Random Slopes Model 

slopes_model <- "
  X =~ x1 + x2 + x3
  Z =~ z1 + z2 + z3
  Y =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  W =~ w1 + w2 + w3
  Y ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
  W ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
"

Continuous Indicators 

fit_slopes_cont <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopes,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_slopes_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          LINEAR
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   127.210
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                50
#>   SRMR                                           0.008
#>   RMSEA                                          0.018
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.860
#>   x2                                             0.683
#>   x3                                             0.772
#>   z1                                             0.839
#>   z2                                             0.694
#>   z3                                             0.759
#>   y1                                             0.838
#>   y2                                             0.726
#>   y3                                             0.750
#>   w1                                             0.840
#>   w2                                             0.695
#>   w3                                             0.771
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.542
#>   W                                              0.553
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.927      0.011   84.795    0.000
#>     x2              0.826      0.012   70.114    0.000
#>     x3              0.879      0.007  124.945    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.916      0.009   99.944    0.000
#>     z2              0.833      0.013   64.045    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.010   91.007    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.915      0.010   88.451    0.000
#>     y2              0.852      0.014   59.806    0.000
#>     y3              0.866      0.012   72.783    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.916      0.012   78.577    0.000
#>     w2              0.834      0.017   47.894    0.000
#>     w3              0.878      0.013   65.860    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.293      0.020   14.941    0.000
#>     Z               0.444      0.035   12.526    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.393      0.038   10.319    0.000
#>     Z               0.248      0.061    4.072    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.010      0.006    1.765    0.077
#>    .W               0.010      0.007    1.386    0.166
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.175      0.013   13.763    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.004      0.006   -0.645    0.519
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.025      0.013   -1.992    0.046
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.007    1.661    0.097
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.003      0.011    0.292    0.770
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.009      0.012    0.748    0.455
#>     W~X             0.013      0.011    1.177    0.239
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.458      0.029   15.951    0.000
#>    .W               0.447      0.037   12.051    0.000
#>    .x1              0.140      0.020    6.908    0.000
#>    .x2              0.317      0.020   16.248    0.000
#>    .x3              0.228      0.012   18.458    0.000
#>    .z1              0.161      0.017    9.564    0.000
#>    .z2              0.306      0.022   14.161    0.000
#>    .z3              0.241      0.017   14.483    0.000
#>    .y1              0.162      0.019    8.564    0.000
#>    .y2              0.274      0.024   11.290    0.000
#>    .y3              0.250      0.021   12.142    0.000
#>    .w1              0.160      0.021    7.496    0.000
#>    .w2              0.305      0.029   10.535    0.000
#>    .w3              0.229      0.023    9.812    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.086      0.019    4.449    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.005    3.521    0.000
#>     Y~Z             0.105      0.015    7.134    0.000
#>     W~1             0.059      0.015    4.079    0.000
#>     W~X             0.095      0.015    6.359    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.144      0.026    5.563    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_slopes_ord <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopesOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomSlopesOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_slopes_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   263.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                50
#>   SRMR                                           0.010
#>   RMSEA                                          0.029
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.870
#>   x2                                             0.669
#>   x3                                             0.789
#>   z1                                             0.841
#>   z2                                             0.714
#>   z3                                             0.758
#>   y1                                             0.844
#>   y2                                             0.711
#>   y3                                             0.754
#>   w1                                             0.835
#>   w2                                             0.681
#>   w3                                             0.785
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.539
#>   W                                              0.550
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.933      0.012   78.084    0.000
#>     x2              0.818      0.012   65.615    0.000
#>     x3              0.888      0.011   81.079    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.917      0.013   70.801    0.000
#>     z2              0.845      0.014   59.089    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.012   75.197    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.918      0.011   86.263    0.000
#>     y2              0.843      0.013   66.650    0.000
#>     y3              0.869      0.013   67.834    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.914      0.010   87.281    0.000
#>     w2              0.825      0.015   56.699    0.000
#>     w3              0.886      0.012   74.971    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.290      0.026   11.109    0.000
#>     Z               0.451      0.038   11.719    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.391      0.037   10.580    0.000
#>     Z               0.245      0.053    4.639    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.011      0.005    2.173    0.030
#>    .W               0.009      0.008    1.104    0.270
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.168      0.011   14.836    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.007      0.006   -1.024    0.306
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.025      0.015   -1.667    0.096
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.008    1.522    0.128
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.004      0.010    0.371    0.711
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.010      0.013    0.741    0.459
#>     W~X             0.016      0.010    1.648    0.099
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.461      0.025   18.252    0.000
#>    .W               0.450      0.033   13.679    0.000
#>    .x1              0.130      0.022    5.873    0.000
#>    .x2              0.331      0.020   16.243    0.000
#>    .x3              0.211      0.020   10.803    0.000
#>    .z1              0.159      0.024    6.629    0.000
#>    .z2              0.286      0.024   11.843    0.000
#>    .z3              0.242      0.020   12.036    0.000
#>    .y1              0.156      0.020    8.009    0.000
#>    .y2              0.289      0.021   13.553    0.000
#>    .y3              0.246      0.022   11.084    0.000
#>    .w1              0.165      0.019    8.675    0.000
#>    .w2              0.319      0.024   13.188    0.000
#>    .w3              0.215      0.021   10.277    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.084      0.021    4.071    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.005    3.827    0.000
#>     Y~Z             0.101      0.016    6.408    0.000
#>     W~1             0.058      0.013    4.487    0.000
#>     W~X             0.099      0.017    5.851    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.142      0.027    5.271    0.000

Random Intercepts Model 

intercepts_model <- '
  f =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  f ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + w1 + w2 + (1 | cluster)
'

Continuous Indicators 

fit_intercepts_cont <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomIntercepts,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_intercepts_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          LINEAR
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    24.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                20
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.005
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.891
#>   y2                                             0.785
#>   y3                                             0.814
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.744
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.944      0.007  131.287    0.000
#>     y2              0.886      0.011   83.441    0.000
#>     y3              0.902      0.008  111.733    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.238      0.007   32.426    0.000
#>     x2              0.162      0.008   21.194    0.000
#>     x3              0.077      0.006   13.262    0.000
#>     w1              0.128      0.042    3.040    0.002
#>     w2              0.091      0.040    2.260    0.024
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.013      0.008   -1.615    0.106
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.104      0.010    9.987    0.000
#>     x3              0.004      0.008    0.528    0.598
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.097      0.010    9.639    0.000
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.041      0.059   -0.690    0.490
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.256      0.016   16.141    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.109      0.014    8.000    0.000
#>    .y2              0.215      0.019   11.374    0.000
#>    .y3              0.186      0.015   12.797    0.000
#>     f~1             0.621      0.022   28.428    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_intercepts_ord <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomInterceptsOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomInterceptsOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_intercepts_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    25.508
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                20
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.005
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.885
#>   y2                                             0.788
#>   y3                                             0.809
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.723
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.941      0.011   85.406    0.000
#>     y2              0.888      0.013   69.538    0.000
#>     y3              0.899      0.012   73.179    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.241      0.009   26.825    0.000
#>     x2              0.158      0.007   23.128    0.000
#>     x3              0.080      0.006   14.542    0.000
#>     w1              0.121      0.041    2.961    0.003
#>     w2              0.081      0.043    1.907    0.057
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.012      0.009   -1.321    0.187
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.110      0.011   10.252    0.000
#>     x3              0.012      0.012    1.014    0.311
#>     w1              0.001      0.003    0.249    0.803
#>     w2              0.001      0.003    0.340    0.734
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.099      0.010    9.771    0.000
#>     w1             -0.003      0.003   -1.119    0.263
#>     w2             -0.001      0.003   -0.415    0.678
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1             -0.003      0.002   -1.276    0.202
#>     w2              0.003      0.004    0.868    0.385
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.025      0.047   -0.526    0.599
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.277      0.015   17.923    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.115      0.021    5.524    0.000
#>    .y2              0.212      0.023    9.319    0.000
#>    .y3              0.191      0.022    8.706    0.000
#>     f~1             0.601      0.021   28.483    0.000
